proof fn phi_3_l1_entry_can_point_to_l1_table(pt: PageTable, p: PageMapPtr, q: PageMapPtr, i: L1Index)
    requires
        pt.wf_l1(),
        pt.l1_tables@.dom().contains(p),
        pt.l1_tables@.dom().contains(q),
        0 <= i < 512,
        pt.l1_tables@[p].value()[i].perm.present,
        pt.l1_tables@[p].value()[i].addr == q,
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: wf_l1 forbids L1 entries from pointing to L2, L3, or cr3 tables but does NOT forbid pointing to other L1 table pages — a data mapping could alias an L1 page table page

name: disjoint_l3_only_non_ps_entries
code:
proof fn phi_4_disjoint_l3_only_non_ps_entries(
    pt: PageTable, pi: PageMapPtr, pj: PageMapPtr, l3i: L3Index, l3j: L3Index
)
    requires
        pt.disjoint_l3(),
        pt.l3_tables@.dom().contains(pi),
        pt.l3_tables@.dom().contains(pj),
        pi != pj,
        0 <= l3i < 512,
        0 <= l3j < 512,
        pt.l3_tables@[pi].value()[l3i].perm.present,
        pt.l3_tables@[pj].value()[l3j].perm.present,
        pt.l3_tables@[pi].value()[l3i].perm.ps,
        pt.l3_tables@[pj].value()[l3j].perm.ps,
        pt.l3_tables@[pi].value()[l3i].addr == pt.l3_tables@[pj].value()[l3j].addr,
    ensures
        true,
{
}
reason: disjoint_l3 only enforces address uniqueness for non-ps (non-hugepage) entries — two different L3 ps entries across different tables can map to the same 1G physical page, allowing aliased 1G mappings

proof fn phi_5_page_closure_missing_cr3(pt: PageTable)
    requires
        pt.wf_l4(),
        pt.l3_tables@.dom().finite(),
        pt.l2_tables@.dom().finite(),
        pt.l1_tables@.dom().finite(),
    ensures
        pt.page_closure().contains(pt.cr3),
{
}
reason: page_closure includes l4_table dom (which is {cr3}), so cr3 is always in the closure — but this means cr3 cannot be used as a data page even though no spec prevents mapping a VA to cr3's physical address via L1 entries
